events.body: Running status against the dbt project. Project parses clean — 18 models, 84 tests, last refresh 06:10 CST.

Running search for 'Hurricane Beryl', 'wind claim', 'cat severity', 'tier-2 commercial', 'debris removal', 'coastal Texas', 'Louisiana wind'. Four upstream models surface: gold.fct_claim, gold.dim_policy, gold.fct_premium, gold.dim_peril.
events.side_effect: tool:search matched gold.fct_claim, gold.dim_policy, gold.fct_premium, gold.dim_peril

events.body: Running describe on each candidate. gold.fct_claim is claim-grain with loss_date, peril_code, policy_id, incurred_loss, and paid_loss. gold.dim_policy carries tier classification, debris_removal_sublimit, state_code, and county_fips. gold.fct_premium tracks cat_load by policy and effective year. gold.dim_peril maps HU-2024-BERYL to Gulf Coast zone flags — that is the peril slice the Reserves Committee needs.
events.side_effect: tool:describe returned grain=claim_id on fct_claim · grain=policy_id on dim_policy · grain=policy_id·effective_year on fct_premium · grain=peril_code on dim_peril

events.body: Running lineage. Join path is clean: claims join to policies on policy_id, policies join to premiums on policy_id and effective_year, perils attach via peril_code. Gap confirmed — no model aggregates claim severity to (policy_tier, region_code, loss_date) for a named CAT event. That is exactly what gold.fct_cat_wind_severity_by_tier_region_daily needs to be.
events.side_effect: tool:lineage validated join path · gap confirmed at tier-region-daily grain

events.body: Spinning up an XS warehouse and running dbt_show on Hurricane-Beryl coastal claims filtered to peril_code = 'HU-2024-BERYL' and state IN ('TX', 'LA'). Tier-2 commercial wind shows avg_severity_ratio of 1.38 versus the model assumption of 1.00 — the 38% excess is real and the grain is viable.
events.side_effect: tool:warehouse start XS · tool:dbt_show HU-2024-BERYL coastal slice · severity_ratio 1.38 vs 1.00 model confirmed

events.body: Authoring models/gold/fct_cat_wind_severity_by_tier_region_daily.sql. Writing the config block first — table materialization, gold schema, tags cat_analytics and ai_built.
Writing the header docblock so actuaries and downstream readers know what this model is and who built it.
events.code_append:
-- gold.fct_cat_wind_severity_by_tier_region_daily
-- CAT wind claim severity versus model assumption, by policy tier and region
-- Grain: one row per (policy_tier, region_code, loss_date)
-- Built live by dbt-wizard sub-agents in response to Reserves Committee escalation
-- Sources: gold.fct_claim + gold.dim_policy + gold.fct_premium + gold.dim_peril

events.body: Anchoring the beryl_claims CTE to Hurricane-Beryl wind claims in Gulf Coast states — that is the denominator for the severity ratio.
events.code_append:
with beryl_claims as (
    select
        c.claim_id,
        c.policy_id,
        c.loss_date,
        c.incurred_loss,
        c.paid_loss
    from {{ ref('fct_claim') }} c
    join {{ ref('dim_peril') }} p on p.peril_code = c.peril_code
    where p.peril_code = 'HU-2024-BERYL'
      and p.gulf_coast_flag = true
),

events.body: Pulling in policy tier, debris_removal_sublimit, state code, and county FIPS from dim_policy. Joining premium to get the cat_load model assumption per policy.
events.code_append:
policy_enriched as (
    select
        bc.claim_id,
        bc.loss_date,
        bc.incurred_loss,
        dp.tier_code         as policy_tier,
        dp.state_code        as region_code,
        dp.debris_removal_sublimit,
        coalesce(fp.cat_load, 1.0) as model_cat_load
    from beryl_claims bc
    join {{ ref('dim_policy') }} dp
      on dp.policy_id = bc.policy_id
     and dp.dbt_valid_to is null
    left join {{ ref('fct_premium') }} fp
      on fp.policy_id = bc.policy_id
     and fp.effective_year = date_part('year', bc.loss_date)
    where dp.coverage_type = 'WIND'
      and dp.tier_code = 'TIER-2'
      and dp.state_code in ('TX', 'LA')
)

events.body: Final SELECT — one row per (policy_tier, region_code, loss_date). Claim count, total incurred loss, average model assumption, actual average severity, and the ratio that quantifies the reserve gap.
events.code_append:
select
    policy_tier,
    region_code,
    loss_date,
    count(*)                                 as claim_count,
    sum(incurred_loss)                        as total_incurred_loss,
    avg(model_cat_load)                       as avg_model_assumption,
    avg(incurred_loss)                        as avg_actual_severity,
    avg(incurred_loss) / nullif(avg(model_cat_load), 0)
                                              as avg_severity_ratio,
    sum(case when incurred_loss / nullif(model_cat_load, 0) > 1.2
             then 1 else 0 end)               as over_model_claims
from policy_enriched
group by 1, 2, 3
order by loss_date desc, region_code, policy_tier

events.body: Writing the YAML companion. Description first, then the schema contract — every column gets a type so actuaries and downstream consumers can resolve it without ambiguity.
events.code_append:
version: 2

models:
  - name: fct_cat_wind_severity_by_tier_region_daily
    description: |
      CAT wind claim severity versus model assumption aggregated to
      (policy_tier, region_code, loss_date). Denominator is all
      Hurricane-Beryl wind claims for Gulf Coast states.
      Built by dbt-wizard sub-agents on 2026-05-23
      in response to Reserves Committee escalation on the tier-2
      coastal wind severity deviation.
    config:
      contract:
        enforced: true
      tags: [cat_analytics, ai_built]
      meta:
        owner: cat_actuarial
        built_by: dbt-wizard
        consumes: [fct_claim, dim_policy, fct_premium, dim_peril]

events.body: Adding column-level tests. Uniqueness on the composite key (policy_tier, region_code, loss_date). Not-null on avg_severity_ratio and claim_count. Accepted values on region_code to catch state-code taxonomy drift.
events.code_append:
columns:
      - name: policy_tier
        data_type: varchar
        tests:
          - not_null
          - accepted_values:
              values: ['TIER-1', 'TIER-2', 'TIER-3', 'TIER-4']
      - name: region_code
        data_type: varchar
        tests:
          - not_null
          - accepted_values:
              values: ['TX', 'LA', 'FL', 'MS', 'AL', 'GA', 'SC', 'NC']
      - name: loss_date
        data_type: date
        tests:
          - not_null
      - name: claim_count
        data_type: number
        tests:
          - not_null
      - name: total_incurred_loss
        data_type: number
      - name: avg_model_assumption
        data_type: number
      - name: avg_actual_severity
        data_type: number
      - name: avg_severity_ratio
        data_type: number
        tests:
          - not_null
      - name: over_model_claims
        data_type: number

    tests:
      - dbt_utils.unique_combination_of_columns:
          combination_of_columns: [policy_tier, region_code, loss_date]

events.body: Running dbt_show on the compiled model against the Hurricane-Beryl slice. All column tests resolve clean. Schema contract enforces. The tier-2 coastal wind severity ratio of 1.38 is visible in the first rows for TX and LA — 22 of 34 over-model claims share the outdated debris-removal sublimit clause.
events.side_effect: tool:dbt_show compiled · tests:passed · contract:enforced · severity_ratio 1.38 confirmed · 22/34 over-model share debris_removal_sublimit clause

events.body: Materializing gold.fct_cat_wind_severity_by_tier_region_daily to Snowflake via the dbt adapter. Writing to the gold schema. Registering with the catalog.

Running lineage one more time. Four upstreams attached. Downstream consumers — cat-loss dashboards, Reserves Committee brief, reinsurance treaty pricing feeds, Snowflake direct queries — can now resolve the new gold table on their next pass. The Reserves Committee's question has a production asset behind it.
events.side_effect: tool:lineage updated · downstream consumers notified · 18 models → 19 models
